steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content: The blank completes “before deciding which link”—creating an indirect question (a question embedded within a larger declarative statement), not a standalone direct question.

steps.title: Apply the rule for indirect questions
steps.content:
Indirect questions within statements must use standard word order (subject + verb: “you should choose,” not inverted “should you choose”) and must end with the same punctuation as the containing sentence (a period here, not a question mark, since the overall sentence is declarative). D correctly applies standard word order with a period. A and C use inverted word order, and B uses a question mark—both features of direct questions, not embedded ones.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
